response:
Alex handles an average of 20 support tickets daily. Breaking this down, 40% are file access issues, which equals 8 tickets (20 * 0.40). Another 35% are data security concerns, totaling 7 tickets (20 * 0.35). The remaining miscellaneous issues make up the rest, calculated as 20 minus 15, which is 5 tickets.

On this specific day, Alex resolved all 8 file access tickets and all 7 data security tickets. For the miscellaneous category, Alex resolved only half, which is 2.5 tickets; however, since support tickets are discrete units, we will interpret this as resolving 2 full tickets and leaving one pending, or simply calculating the mathematical sum as 2.5 for the sake of the total count if fractional resolution is allowed in the context of the problem. Assuming standard integer counts for resolved tickets, Alex resolved 2 of the 5 miscellaneous tickets.

The total number of resolved tickets is the sum of the resolved file access (8), data security (7), and miscellaneous (2) tickets, which equals 17.

Navigating through the **highway** of technical support can be demanding, but Alex managed a great volume of work. Even if this busy day was right before a **holiday**, the efficiency was impressive. It is clear that Alex is well-prepared for the upcoming **holiday** season, having cleared a significant backlog. In total, Alex resolved 17 support tickets on that day.
